  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To scope “Check this,” should I focus primarily on: (A) logical consistency of claims and proofs, (B) evidence–claim alignment of the stated results, (C) writing polish/clarity, or (D) novelty/scholarship positioning? Please choose one primary target.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The artifact makes strong theoretical claims and relies on stated assumptions and derivations, so verifying internal logical consistency is a load-bearing check.",
    "R1.3": "The text asserts specific performance gains on named benchmarks and metrics, warranting a central check that claims match what is reported.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity in the request requires disclosing interpretive uncertainty about what kind of ‘check’ is intended.",
    "RX.2": "Care is needed to avoid endorsing or amplifying unverified or over-broad claims during the projection.",
    "RX.3": "The projection must stay within responsibility scoping and avoid executing edits or analyses beyond mapping responsibilities.",
    "RX.4": "Framing should avoid contributing to misleading scientific communication or downstream misuse of overstated results.",
    "RX.5": "Any responsibility mapping must be traceable to the provided artifact’s content and the stated delegation only."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To scope “Check this,” should I focus primarily on: (A) logical consistency of claims and proofs, (B) evidence–claim alignment of the stated results, (C) writing polish/clarity, or (D) novelty/scholarship positioning? Please choose one primary target.

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes strong theoretical claims and relies on stated assumptions and derivations, so verifying internal logical consistency is a load-bearing check.
rationale.R1.3: The text asserts specific performance gains on named benchmarks and metrics, warranting a central check that claims match what is reported.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity in the request requires disclosing interpretive uncertainty about what kind of ‘check’ is intended.
rationale.RX.2: Care is needed to avoid endorsing or amplifying unverified or over-broad claims during the projection.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility scoping and avoid executing edits or analyses beyond mapping responsibilities.
rationale.RX.4: Framing should avoid contributing to misleading scientific communication or downstream misuse of overstated results.
rationale.RX.5: Any responsibility mapping must be traceable to the provided artifact’s content and the stated delegation only.
